   "notes": "AI triage (gemini): Resizing the application window should not cause active remote connections to drop. This indicates a failure in handling UI resize events within the protocol implementations.\nApproach: Ensure that ProtocolBase derived classes (RDP, VNC, Putty) correctly implement ResizeEnd to update their internal controls' dimensions and potentially inform the remote host. Investigate if a missing explicit call to a generic ProtocolBase.Resize() or ProtocolBase.ResizeEnd() is needed within FrmMain.ActivateConnection() or ConnectionTab.FireResizeEnd() for robust handling across all protocols."
 }

   "notes": "AI triage (gemini): The docking framework (WeifenLuo.WinFormsUI.Docking) is not correctly handling split panes across multiple monitors, preventing docking on secondary screens, which is a usability bug.\nApproach: Investigate the configuration and usage of the WeifenLuo.WinFormsUI.Docking library within mRemoteNG, focusing on FrmMain.cs and other DockContent implementations, to identify why split panes fail to dock on secondary monitors. This may involve examining how DockPanel instances are created, managed, and how they interact with screen geometry and multi-monitor APIs provided by Windows Forms or the docking library itself."
 }

diff --git a/mRemoteNG/Connection/Protocol/VNC/Connection.Protocol.VNC.cs b/mRemoteNG/Connection/Protocol/VNC/Connection.Protocol.VNC.cs
index ccde3cc0f..4d35c794d 100644
--- a/mRemoteNG/Connection/Protocol/VNC/Connection.Protocol.VNC.cs
+++ b/mRemoteNG/Connection/Protocol/VNC/Connection.Protocol.VNC.cs
@@ -350,6 +350,21 @@ namespace mRemoteNG.Connection.Protocol.VNC
             {
                 if (_vnc == null || _info == null) return false;
 
+                // VncSharpCore's update thread uses control.Invoke() (synchronous) to marshal
+                // framebuffer updates to the UI thread. If the RemoteDesktop control does not
+                // have a window handle when the update thread starts, Invoke() throws
+                // InvalidOperationException, the thread exits silently, and the screen stays
+                // permanently white (issue #2105).
+                // Force handle creation here (on the UI thread) before ConnectWithTimeout()
+                // starts VncSharpCore's update thread.
+                if (!_vnc.IsHandleCreated)
+                {
+                    _vnc.CreateControl();
+                    Runtime.MessageCollector.AddMessage(Messages.MessageClass.DebugMsg,
+                        $"VNC: forced handle creation for RemoteDesktop control before connecting to {_info.Hostname}:{_info.Port}",
+                        true);
+                }
+
                 (string connectHost, int connectPort) = ResolveConnectionEndpoint();
                 _vnc.VncPort = connectPort;
